data.title: GEOPOLITICS | European Nations Discuss Seizing Russian-Linked Oil Tankers
data.description: British Defense Secretary John Healey convened with defense officials from Baltic and Nordic countries to deliberate on the potential seizure of oil tankers associated with Russia's shadow fleet. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Europe's efforts to intensify restrictions on Moscow's war economy. The meeting underscores the region's commitment to curbing Russia's economic activities amid ongoing geopolitical tensions.

data.title: Enbridge Declines New Oil Pipeline Development to Canada's West Coast
data.description: Enbridge has announced its decision not to pursue the development of a new oil pipeline from Alberta to Canada's West Coast. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the company's executives discussed this stance during an earnings call. The decision reflects Enbridge's unwillingness to assume the associated development risks. This move comes amid ongoing discussions about energy infrastructure and environmental considerations in the region.

data.title: Valentine's Day Animation Gains Popularity
data.description: Crypto Jargon posted on X. A new Valentine's Day-themed animation has captured the attention of users, gaining popularity across social media platforms. The animation, which features romantic elements, has been praised for its creativity and effectiveness in conveying the spirit of Valentine's Day. Users have expressed their appreciation for the animation, noting its ability to resonate with audiences and enhance the festive atmosphere of the holiday.

data.title: BTC miner IREN opens in the green, riding AI momentum to MSCI inclusion
data.description:
IREN has announced that it will be added to the MSCI USA Index, a major benchmark that tracks the performance of large and mid-cap US stocks, by the end of February. The inclusion is expected to boost IREN’s visibility among institutional investors and index-tracking funds, which may support the company’s long-term price and capital-raising plans. […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/iren-green-riding-ai-momentum-msci-inclusion/

data.title: S&P 500 Earnings Growth Led by Tech and Industrials in Q4
data.description: Nine out of eleven sectors in the S&P 500 are experiencing earnings growth for the fourth quarter, with the Information Technology and Industrials sectors leading the way. FactSet posted on X, highlighting the performance of these sectors during the earnings season. The growth in these sectors is contributing significantly to the overall positive earnings trend observed in the S&P 500 index. This development underscores the resilience and strength of these industries amid varying economic conditions. The report provides insights into the factors driving this growth and the potential implications for investors and market dynamics.

data.title: Microsoft's AI Data Centers May Benefit from Superconducting Cables
data.description: Microsoft is investigating the application of high-temperature superconducting cables to enhance electrical efficiency and density in its AI data centers. According to NS3.AI, this technological advancement could expedite the expansion of Microsoft's U.S. data centers by minimizing infrastructure requirements and environmental impact. Concurrently, Wall Street maintains a positive outlook on Microsoft, with price targets set between $600 and $650, driven by its strategic advancements in AI and cloud technologies.

data.title: International Skating Union Supports French Judge's Decision in Ice Dance Competition
data.description: The International Skating Union (ISU) has expressed its support for the French judge involved in the recent ice dance competition. New York Times posted on X that the judge favored French ice dancers Laurence Fournier Beaudry and Guillaume Cizeron over American competitors Madison Chock and Evan Bates by nearly eight points in the free skate segment. The decision has sparked discussions within the skating community, highlighting the subjective nature of judging in the sport. The ISU's stance underscores its commitment to maintaining the integrity of the judging process, despite differing opinions on the outcome.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Gold and Silver Futures Set to Rise Amid Market Volatility
data.description: Gold and silver futures are expected to record gains this week, with gold's near-month contract rising by 1.4% and silver's by 1.5%. According to Jin10, gold has seen increases in eight of the past ten weeks, while silver has ended a two-week decline, marking gains in eleven of the past fifteen weeks. Antonio Di Giacomo from XS.com noted in a report that despite volatility in the precious metals market, the demand for safe-haven assets continues to support the rise in gold and silver prices.

data.title: New York City Initiates Guaranteed Income Program for Homeless Youth
data.description: New York City has introduced a $1.5 million guaranteed income pilot program aimed at supporting homeless young adults aged 18 to 24. According to NS3.AI, the initiative provides monthly payments of $1,200 to 60 participants, with the option to receive a $5,000 lump sum payment. The program is designed to offer flexibility and promote self-sufficiency among the participants. Funded by the City Council and Covenant House New York, the program is set to run through June 2026.

data.title: New York Begins Handing $1,200 To Residents Per Month in Guaranteed Income Pilot Program – Here’s Who Will Receive the No Strings Attached Payments
data.description:
New York City has officially launched a guaranteed income pilot program to test whether cash handouts can help people achieve self-sufficiency and stability. The $1. 5 million initiative is giving monthly no-strings-attached payments of $1,200 to young adults aged 18 through 24 who have struggled with homelessness
source: https://dailyhodl.com/2026/02/13/new-york-begins-handing-1200-to-residents-per-month-in-guaranteed-income-pilot-program-heres-who-will-receive-the-no-strings-attached-payments/
